original_description: Agent decision-quality protocol — expect/capture/grade primitives for checking whether the right tool, approach, or research target was chosen at workflow phase transitions. Use for: phase gates between workflow stages, decision routing eval harnesses, transcript grading, or embedding checkpoints in skills. NOT for: pytest/unit tests, ML metrics, LLM output eval, performance benchmarks, or code review — this evaluates agent decisions, not code correctness.

train_results.query: writing a decision routing eval — agents keep picking foxhound when they should use get_docs, want to score the difference systematically
we're instrumenting our skills pipeline with quality gates between stages, specifically to catch wrong-approach selections before they propagate
eval-protocol for checking if subagent chose the right db query strategy at the planning→execution phase gate
need decision-quality checkpoints in our multi-agent pipeline — specifically catching when the agent should have searched mulch but went straight to implementation
I want to add phase gate checks between the research and implementation stages of my skill — capture whether the agent picked the right tool before moving forward
building an eval harness to grade agent decision quality at workflow transitions, need the expect/capture/grade primitives
run the autoresearch audit to gate my new skill against the holistic integration principle
check my executing-plans phase transitions — the plan has 4 stages and I want to make sure each checkpoint commit captures the right state
writing characterization tests for the existing routing code so I have a safety net before I refactor it
I want to evaluate whether my agents are making good decisions — should I use cognitive-guardrails or something else for bias checks at key moments
performance benchmark for our decision tree — want to know latency at each routing branch under load
audit my skill's cognitive checkpoints — specifically the wysiati and overconfidence checks to make sure they fire at the right decision points
